name: Mine production traces into failure-mode clusters
description: Turn raw production agent runs into ranked, named failure-mode clusters that point to what to fix next.
general_steps: Sample recent production traces|Group by failure type|Rank clusters by frequency x severity|Link representative exemplars|File novel failures to the eval dataset|Hand off the ranked queue
reasoning_model: Failing traces are the roadmap; cluster into patterns, never react to one-offs; the production distribution always differs from staging.
common_pitfalls: Vibe-checking logs|reacting to one dramatic trace|ignoring novel modes|not growing the dataset
ai_opportunity: Auto-cluster low-scoring traces into a triage queue; PM prioritizes which clusters matter.

name: Run a capability-elicitation probe on a new model
description: Test whether a newly released model unlocks a use case the current model cannot do.
general_steps: Define candidate task + inputs|Prompt across regimes (zero/few-shot, tools)|Score vs the incumbent model|Note capability + failure deltas|Decide unlock yes/no/needs-work
reasoning_model: A new model is an opportunity surface, not just a swap; probe for net-new capability before re-pricing the roadmap.
common_pitfalls: Anecdotal one-prompt judgments|testing on memorized examples|ignoring cost/latency of the new capability
ai_opportunity: AI runs the probe battery and scores; PM judges whether the unlocked capability is worth building on.

name: Triage a thumbs-down or negative-feedback event
description: Classify a single user-flagged bad output and route it to a fix or the dataset.
general_steps: Read the flagged trace|Reproduce with seed/temperature|Classify the failure type|Add to the eval/regression dataset|Route to prompt/retrieval/tool/model owner
reasoning_model: A thumbs-down is a labeled datapoint, not a complaint; reproduce before believing and capture it before it decays.
common_pitfalls: Treating it as a one-off|not reproducing|losing the trace link|no dataset capture
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the classification + repro and proposes the route; PM confirms severity.

name: Build a use-case to eval-gap map
description: Map desired product behaviors to existing evals and flag the ungraded ones before they regress.
general_steps: Enumerate target use cases/behaviors|Map each to an existing eval|Flag behaviors with no coverage|Size the risk of each gap|Prioritize gaps to close
reasoning_model: Ungraded behavior is unmanaged behavior; coverage gaps are where silent regressions hide.
common_pitfalls: Assuming a passing suite means full coverage|ignoring slices|no risk sizing
ai_opportunity: AI inventories behaviors and cross-references the eval suite; PM ranks the gaps.

name: Competitive model or agent teardown on your eval set
description: Run a competitor model or agent through your own rubric to locate capability deltas.
general_steps: Get competitor access|Run your eval set through it|Score vs your system|Note where it wins/loses|Decide borrow/counter/ignore
reasoning_model: Your eval set is the only fair comparison; public leaderboards measure someone else's task, not yours.
common_pitfalls: Trusting public benchmark scores|definition mismatch|copying capability without the use case
ai_opportunity: AI runs the competitor through the harness and tabulates deltas; PM judges the strategic response.

name: Cluster negative feedback into quality themes
description: Cluster thumbs-down, edits, and complaints into ranked quality themes for the roadmap.
general_steps: Pull the feedback window|Cluster by theme|Rank by frequency x severity|Link exemplars|Tag novel themes
reasoning_model: The score is noise; the verbatims and edits are the signal about what to fix.
common_pitfalls: Fixating on the aggregate score|over-reading a small sample|losing source links
ai_opportunity: AI clusters and ranks; PM validates the themes.

name: Mine tool-call logs for unreliable tools
description: Find tools or MCP servers with high error, timeout, or misuse rates from trace spans.
general_steps: Pull tool-call spans|Compute error+latency+timeout by tool|Rank the unreliable tools|Flag flaky/abused tools|Hand off for fix or replacement
reasoning_model: A flaky tool quietly caps agent quality; reliability of the tool surface is a product metric, not just an ops one.
common_pitfalls: Treating tool errors as pure ops|ignoring silent timeouts|no per-tool attribution
ai_opportunity: AI aggregates tool-call spans and ranks reliability; PM decides fix vs replace.

name: Write the eval rubric or scoring function for a capability
description: Define what good means for an AI capability as a graded scoring function; the eval is the modern PRD.
general_steps: Assemble real example inputs|Define the task the system performs|Write explicit one-dimension scoring criteria|Encode graded scores|Calibrate against human judgment|Version the rubric
reasoning_model: The scoring function is the new PRD; the prompt is temporary, the eval is permanent; score one dimension per scorer or you cannot localize a regression.
common_pitfalls: Delegating the rubric to engineers|vibe-checking instead of scoring|lumping multiple dimensions|no real-data examples
ai_opportunity: AI drafts candidate criteria + a starter dataset; the PM personally owns and calibrates the rubric.

name: Curate a golden eval dataset from prod traces
description: Build the versioned dataset of representative and hard cases the eval suite runs against.
general_steps: Sample representative + hard + adversarial cases|De-dup|Label expected behavior|Check leakage vs training|Version and freeze the split
reasoning_model: The dataset is the new user research; a frozen, representative split is what makes scores comparable over time.
common_pitfalls: Over-fitting the set to today's prompt|leakage into training|stale set that drifts from prod
ai_opportunity: AI proposes candidate cases from traces; PM curates and labels the gold.

name: Design a single-dimension code-based scorer
description: Write a deterministic scorer that checks exactly one quality dimension (format, schema, exact-match).
general_steps: Pick one dimension|Write the deterministic check|Unit-test the scorer on known cases|Register it in the suite
reasoning_model: Use code for what code can check; one dimension per scorer keeps optimization trade-offs visible.
common_pitfalls: Lumping dimensions|scoring quality as one number|untested scorer
ai_opportunity: AI writes the scorer code from the dimension spec; PM verifies it measures the right thing.

name: Design an LLM-as-judge scorer and rubric
description: Build an LLM-judge scorer for nuanced qualities code cannot check (tone, faithfulness, helpfulness).
general_steps: Write the judge prompt with good/bad examples|Use chain-of-thought scoring|Choose the judge model (often different from task model)|Pin the version
reasoning_model: A judge prompt is product knowledge, not engineering; vague judge prompts produce vague scores; the judge can itself be wrong.
common_pitfalls: Vague criteria|judge equals task model with same blind spots|no examples|unpinned judge drifting
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the judge prompt + rubric; PM owns the criteria and must calibrate it.

name: Calibrate an LLM judge against human labels
description: Measure and maintain judge-to-human agreement so eval scores keep reflecting real quality.
general_steps: Sample judged items|Collect human labels|Compute agreement (e.g., kappa)|Adjust the judge prompt|Re-measure on a holdout|Schedule recurring recalibration
reasoning_model: Without calibration, scoring drift silently inflates or deflates results; the judge is an instrument that needs re-zeroing.
common_pitfalls: Trusting an uncalibrated judge|one-time calibration|no drift monitoring
ai_opportunity: AI runs the agreement computation and proposes prompt fixes; PM owns the human-label ground truth.

name: Run an eval-coverage gap analysis
description: Find behaviors and slices with no automated grader before they regress in production.
general_steps: List behaviors + slices|Map to evals|Flag gaps|Size each gap's risk|Recommend new evals
reasoning_model: You can only safely automate what you can grade; coverage is the precondition for autonomy.
common_pitfalls: Assuming a green suite equals covered|ignoring rare-but-severe slices
ai_opportunity: AI cross-references behaviors against the suite; PM ranks gaps by risk.

name: Build slice and segment evals
description: Score the system by input type, length, language, and user segment to catch localized regressions.
general_steps: Define slices|Split the dataset|Run per-slice scoring|Flag the worst slices|Track slice trend
reasoning_model: An aggregate pass can hide a broken slice; quality is a distribution, read it by segment.
common_pitfalls: Aggregate-only reporting|under-powered slices|ignoring language/locale
ai_opportunity: AI computes per-slice scores; PM judges which slices matter.

name: Version and changelog an eval suite
description: Snapshot and changelog the eval suite so score movements are attributable to suite vs system changes.
general_steps: Snapshot the suite|Diff vs prior version|Note threshold/criteria changes|Tag the release
reasoning_model: If the ruler changes silently you cannot tell improvement from measurement drift; version the ruler.
common_pitfalls: Silent threshold changes|untracked dataset edits|no changelog
ai_opportunity: AI snapshots and diffs the suite automatically into a changelog.

name: Define the release quality bar (eval thresholds)
description: Pre-commit the exact eval thresholds that decide ship/hold for any model or prompt change.
general_steps: Pick the gating metrics|Set pass thresholds + CIs|Set guardrail floors (safety, groundedness)|Lock the bar before the change|Document it
reasoning_model: Pre-register the bar before data exists to prevent motivated reasoning at ship time; the gate is the eval, not a vibe.
common_pitfalls: Setting the bar after seeing results|no guardrail floors|moving goalposts
ai_opportunity: AI proposes thresholds from history; PM owns and locks the bar.

name: Author adversarial and regression eval cases from an incident
description: Turn a production failure into a permanent regression test so it never silently returns.
general_steps: Take the failing trace|Minimize a reliable repro|Encode expected behavior|Add to the regression set|Confirm it fails pre-fix and passes post-fix
reasoning_model: Every incident is a free eval case; a fixed bug without a regression test is a bug waiting to recur.
common_pitfalls: Fixing without a regression case|non-reproducible case|over-specific case that never re-triggers
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the regression case from the trace; PM confirms it captures the real failure.

name: Run the weekly eval-score review
description: Hold a weekly team review of eval-score movement and regressions, not a feature demo.
general_steps: Pull the week's eval scores|Flag regressions + wins|Tie each move to a change|Assign follow-ups|Update the bar if needed
reasoning_model: The flywheel is the new sprint cycle; demo scores weekly so regressions surface in days, not quarters.
common_pitfalls: Demoing features instead of scores|no owner per regression|ignoring slow drift
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the weekly score delta + candidate causes; PM runs the judgment.

name: Author and version a prompt in the registry
description: Write and version a prompt as a tracked artifact linked to its eval.
general_steps: Draft the prompt|Diff vs current|Tag a version|Link the governing eval|Register
reasoning_model: Prompts are code; version them, link them to an eval, and never edit prod prompts in place.
common_pitfalls: Editing prod prompts untracked|no eval link|prompt sprawl
ai_opportunity: AI drafts and diffs prompt versions; PM owns intent and the eval link.

name: Prompt regression test before merge
description: Run a candidate prompt against the eval set and block the merge on any score regression.
general_steps: Run candidate vs baseline on the eval set|Compare scores per dimension|Block on regression|Report deltas
reasoning_model: A one-line prompt edit is a distribution change; gate it like code, because it can silently regress everything.
common_pitfalls: Shipping prompt edits ungated|eyeballing a few outputs|ignoring slice regressions
ai_opportunity: Runs unattended as a CI gate; blocks regressions automatically.

name: Context-window budget design
description: Decide what enters the context window and at what token cost for a given task.
general_steps: Inventory context sources|Rank by value per token|Set the token budget|Test truncation/eviction behavior|Eval quality vs budget
reasoning_model: Context is a scarce, paid resource; more tokens is not more quality past the point of dilution.
common_pitfalls: Stuffing context|ignoring cost per token|no eval on truncation
ai_opportunity: AI proposes the budget and tests truncation; PM sets the value ranking.

name: Design or iterate a RAG retrieval pipeline
description: Design chunking, embeddings, and reranking for retrieval-augmented generation.
general_steps: Choose chunking strategy|Pick the embedding model|Add a reranker|Wire retrieved context to the prompt|Eval end-to-end
reasoning_model: Retrieval quality caps answer quality; a wrong chunk is a confident wrong answer.
common_pitfalls: Chunking by bytes not meaning|no reranker|ignoring retrieval recall
ai_opportunity: AI iterates the pipeline configs; PM owns the quality target.

name: Eval retrieval quality
description: Measure recall@k and the groundedness of retrieved context independently of the generator.
general_steps: Build a retrieval eval set|Measure recall@k|Measure answer groundedness on retrieved context|Flag low-recall queries
reasoning_model: Separate retrieval failures from generation failures, or you will tune the wrong half.
common_pitfalls: Only evaluating the final answer|no recall measurement|ignoring grounding
ai_opportunity: AI computes recall@k and groundedness; PM judges acceptable retrieval.

name: Design a tool or function schema
description: Specify the tool contract the model calls: inputs, outputs, errors, and model-legible descriptions.
general_steps: Define inputs/outputs|Specify error semantics|Write a description the model can act on|Test with the model|Version the schema
reasoning_model: The tool description is a prompt; design for the model's job, and make errors part of the contract.
common_pitfalls: Human-centric naming|undefined errors|ambiguous descriptions the model misuses
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the schema + description; PM/eng confirm the contract.

name: Eval tool-call accuracy
description: Measure whether the model selects the right tool with the right arguments.
general_steps: Build a tool-use eval set|Score tool selection|Score argument accuracy|Flag systematic misuse
reasoning_model: Tool-use is a distinct capability with its own benchmark; an agent that calls the wrong tool well is still wrong.
common_pitfalls: Only scoring final answer|ignoring arg errors|no negative (should-not-call) cases
ai_opportunity: AI scores selection + args against gold; PM curates the scenarios.

name: Select or evaluate an MCP server (governance + reliability)
description: Evaluate and approve an MCP server for reliability, schema quality, and security before wiring it in.
general_steps: Check provenance + governance (managed vs shadow)|Test schema + reliability|Eval tool-call success rate|Review security/permission surface|Approve or reject
reasoning_model: An MCP server is third-party code in your agent loop; unvetted servers bypass security and audit controls.
common_pitfalls: Installing shadow/unmanaged servers|trusting marketing over testing|ignoring permission scope
ai_opportunity: AI tests reliability + schema; PM/security own the governance approval.

name: Design the agent loop or harness
description: Define the plan-act-observe loop, termination conditions, and tool-call ceilings for the agent.
general_steps: Define the loop structure|Set stop/termination conditions|Set max steps + token ceiling|Handle tool errors + retries|Spec memory hooks
reasoning_model: The harness is the product; unbounded loops burn money and an agent with no stop condition fails unsafely.
common_pitfalls: No termination condition|unbounded tool-calls|silent infinite loops|no error handling
ai_opportunity: AI proposes loop + guards; PM/eng own the architecture and limits.

name: Set the agent step, tool-call, and token budget
description: Bound cost and loops per run with explicit step, tool-call, and token ceilings.
general_steps: Set max tool-calls|Set max tokens per run|Set a per-run cost cap|Add a loop-guard|Alert on cap hits
reasoning_model: Every run has a cost ceiling or it has none; the budget is a safety and an economics control at once.
common_pitfalls: No per-run cap|runaway loops|no alert on cap breaches
ai_opportunity: AI recommends budgets from run telemetry; PM sets the ceiling.

name: Run a model bake-off on your eval set
description: Select a model by running candidates through your own rubric, not public leaderboards.
general_steps: Pick candidate models|Run your eval suite on each|Compare quality/cost/latency|Note slice differences|Recommend the model
reasoning_model: Choose on your task, not someone else's benchmark; the cheapest model that clears your bar wins.
common_pitfalls: Choosing by leaderboard|ignoring cost/latency|single-slice comparison
ai_opportunity: AI runs the bake-off matrix; PM weighs the cost/quality/latency trade.

name: Design a model routing policy
description: Route easy tasks to a cheap model and hard tasks to a strong one, with a fallback chain.
general_steps: Define the routing signal|Set thresholds|Define the fallback chain|Eval routed vs single-model|Monitor route mix
reasoning_model: Routing is the cost-quality lever; most traffic is easy, so do not pay frontier prices for it.
common_pitfalls: Routing without an eval|no fallback|routing signal that misclassifies hard tasks
ai_opportunity: AI proposes and evaluates the policy; PM owns the cost/quality target.

name: Run a prompt-optimization pass (DSPy/GEPA)
description: Auto-optimize a prompt against the eval metric and validate the gain on a holdout.
general_steps: Define the optimization metric|Run the optimizer|Validate on held-out data|Diff vs the hand-written prompt|Promote or discard
reasoning_model: Optimize against the eval, not vibes; an optimizer that overfits the dev set is a regression in disguise.
common_pitfalls: Overfitting the dev set|no holdout validation|optimizing a mis-specified metric
ai_opportunity: The optimizer runs the search; PM validates the holdout gain.

name: Design agent memory
description: Decide what persists across turns/runs, how it is retrieved, and how it is bounded and protected.
general_steps: Define memory scope|Set write/read policy|Define staleness/eviction|Address privacy of stored memory|Eval retrieval relevance
reasoning_model: Memory is state with privacy and staleness risk; stale or leaked memory is worse than none.
common_pitfalls: Unbounded memory growth|stale memory poisoning answers|storing PII without controls
ai_opportunity: AI proposes the memory design; PM owns scope, privacy, and eviction.

name: Design a preference-data collection task
description: Define the comparison units (prompts, response pairs, ranking rules) that feed RLHF/DPO.
general_steps: Define the prompt distribution|Choose a pairing strategy|Source candidate responses|Define the comparison schema|Pilot with a small batch
reasoning_model: Preference data is the product spec encoded as comparisons; garbage pairs train a garbage reward.
common_pitfalls: Unrepresentative prompts|ambiguous comparisons|no pilot
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the task design + schema; PM owns what preferred means.

name: Write annotation and rater guidelines
description: Author the rubric humans (and AI judges) follow when labeling preferences or quality.
general_steps: Define criteria|Give good/bad examples|Write tie-break rules|Cover edge cases|Version the guideline
reasoning_model: Inconsistent raters train a noisy reward; the guideline is the calibration source for both humans and AI judges.
common_pitfalls: Vague criteria|no examples|no tie-break rules
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the guideline from examples; PM owns the definition of good.

name: Recruit and calibrate raters; measure inter-rater agreement
description: Recruit, qualify, and calibrate human raters and track inter-rater agreement.
general_steps: Recruit/qualify raters|Run a calibration set|Compute inter-rater agreement|Re-train low-agreement raters|Manage the vendor
reasoning_model: If raters disagree the reward learns noise; agreement is the quality floor of the whole training loop.
common_pitfalls: No calibration set|ignoring low agreement|unmanaged vendor quality variance
ai_opportunity: AI computes agreement + flags outliers; PM/vendor own rater quality.

name: Audit preference-data quality
description: Catch noisy, biased, or gamed labels before they train the reward model.
general_steps: Sample labels|Re-label a gold subset|Flag drift/bias|Quarantine bad batches|Report quality
reasoning_model: A bad label compounds through the reward into the policy; audit before you train, not after.
common_pitfalls: Trusting vendor labels blind|no gold re-label|ignoring batch effects
ai_opportunity: AI flags suspicious labels + computes drift; PM sets the quality bar.

name: Spec a reward-model train and eval
description: Define what the reward model should reward and how its quality is independently tested.
general_steps: Define the reward target|Pick the training data|Spec a held-out RM eval|Set acceptance criteria|Plan portability for online RL/eval
reasoning_model: A reward model is a portable scorer; spec its eval as rigorously as the policy's, or it will reward the wrong thing.
common_pitfalls: No held-out RM eval|reward target misaligned with product|untested reward
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the train/eval spec; PM owns the reward definition.

name: Detect reward hacking and reward-model gaming
description: Find where the policy exploits the reward to get high scores with low real quality.
general_steps: Inspect high-reward/low-quality samples|Probe known exploits|Quantify the gap|Patch the reward/verifier|Re-test
reasoning_model: Any reward will be gamed; the question is whether you find the exploit before your users do.
common_pitfalls: Trusting the reward number|no adversarial probing|patching symptoms not the reward
ai_opportunity: AI surfaces high-reward/low-quality divergences; PM judges the exploit.

name: Design an RLAIF or Constitutional preference pipeline
description: Use an AI judge to label routine preferences and route only ambiguous or high-stakes cases to humans.
general_steps: Write the constitution/judge rubric|Generate AI preference labels|Route ambiguous cases to humans|Validate AI labels vs human on a sample|Monitor label quality
reasoning_model: AI labels are far cheaper than human pairs and comparable on clear cases; reserve humans for the hard, high-stakes ones.
common_pitfalls: Circular AI-judging-AI on ambiguous cases|no human validation|over-trusting AI labels on high-stakes
ai_opportunity: The pipeline auto-labels at scale; PM owns the constitution + the human-routing rule.

name: Curate an SFT dataset
description: Assemble supervised fine-tuning examples from prod traces and expert demonstrations.
general_steps: Select demonstrations|Clean and format|De-dup vs the eval set|Label quality|Version
reasoning_model: SFT data quality sets the ceiling; a few hundred excellent examples beat a noisy pile.
common_pitfalls: Leakage into eval|low-quality demos|format inconsistency
ai_opportunity: AI proposes candidate examples; PM curates the gold demonstrations.

name: Plan a distillation run and eval gate
description: Plan distilling a strong teacher into a cheaper student, gated on an eval acceptance bar.
general_steps: Pick teacher + student|Generate the distillation set|Set eval acceptance|Run + eval the student|Gate the ship
reasoning_model: Distillation trades quality for cost; gate on the eval or you ship a quietly worse model to save money.
common_pitfalls: No eval gate|distilling a biased teacher|ignoring slice regressions
ai_opportunity: AI runs the distill + eval; PM owns the quality-vs-cost acceptance.

name: Decide fine-tune vs prompt vs RAG
description: Make the build-method call for a capability gap: prompt, retrieve, or train.
general_steps: Frame the capability gap|Estimate cost/latency/quality of each path|Check data availability|Weigh maintenance burden|Recommend and document
reasoning_model: Fine-tuning is the last resort, not the first; for an orchestrator the answer is usually prompt/RAG until evals prove otherwise.
common_pitfalls: Fine-tuning prematurely|ignoring data cost|no eval evidence for the choice
ai_opportunity: AI models the trade-offs; PM owns the build-vs-train decision.

name: Author an RL environment (dataset + harness + reward)
description: Author a verifiable environment for training/eval; an environment and an agent eval are the same object.
general_steps: Define task inputs (dataset)|Spec the harness/sandbox + tools|Write the reward/rubric|Version as a module|Publish to the env registry
reasoning_model: An RL environment and an agent eval are the same thing - dataset + harness + scoring rules; build it once, use it for both.
common_pitfalls: Treating envs and evals as separate|non-reproducible harness|reward that is easy to game
ai_opportunity: AI scaffolds the env module; PM owns the task definition + reward.

name: Design a sandbox or tool emulator for an environment
description: Build deterministic, isolated stand-ins for real tools so the environment is safe and reproducible.
general_steps: Enumerate the real tools|Build deterministic mocks|Seed environment state|Verify isolation|Validate fidelity vs real tools
reasoning_model: Training/eval needs determinism and isolation; a sandbox that drifts from reality teaches the wrong lesson.
common_pitfalls: Non-deterministic mocks|leaky isolation|emulator that diverges from prod tools
ai_opportunity: AI generates the mocks; PM/eng verify fidelity and isolation.

name: Design and anti-game a reward or verifier function
description: Make high reward actually mean good by red-teaming the reward against exploits.
general_steps: Define a verifiable signal|List likely exploits|Add anti-gaming checks|Red-team the reward|Lock and version
reasoning_model: Verifiable rewards (RLVR) are powerful and brittle; the reward is a spec the policy will attack relentlessly.
common_pitfalls: Easily-gamed reward|reward proxy diverging from quality|no adversarial test
ai_opportunity: AI proposes the reward + probes exploits; PM owns the verifiable definition.

name: Generate and validate synthetic data
description: Generate synthetic cases and prove they are useful and not contamination.
general_steps: Define the generation recipe|Generate cases|Validate quality + diversity|Check leakage vs the eval set|Accept or discard
reasoning_model: Unvalidated synthetic data is contamination wearing a costume; validate diversity and leakage before use.
common_pitfalls: Mode-collapsed generations|leakage into eval|no diversity check
ai_opportunity: AI generates + self-checks; PM validates utility and leakage.

name: Run a train/eval contamination and leakage check
description: Detect overlap between training data and the eval set so scores are not inflated.
general_steps: Hash/embed both sets|Compute overlap/near-dupes|Flag contaminated cases|Quarantine|Re-split clean
reasoning_model: A contaminated eval flatters you into shipping a worse model; clean separation is non-negotiable.
common_pitfalls: Exact-match-only checks (missing near-dupes)|ignoring template leakage|no re-split
ai_opportunity: Runs as an automated leakage gate over train/eval splits.

name: Wire the data flywheel (prod trace to dataset/eval/train)
description: Stand up the standing pipeline that turns production traces into eval/training data.
general_steps: Define capture-to-dataset rules|Set a sampling policy|Scrub PII/consent|Route to eval (now) and train (later)|Set the cadence
reasoning_model: Production traces are the next dataset; the flywheel is the compounding asset, and its leak is PII.
common_pitfalls: No sampling policy|PII captured without consent|capturing without ever using it
ai_opportunity: AI proposes the sampling + scrub rules; PM owns consent/PII policy.

name: Configure eval-gated CI
description: Block merges that drop eval scores below threshold via a CI gate.
general_steps: Wire the eval suite to PRs|Set the thresholds|Fail the build on regression|Report which cases regressed|Require override sign-off
reasoning_model: The eval is the unit test for non-deterministic systems; if it is not in CI, it is not a gate.
common_pitfalls: Eval suite not in CI|thresholds too loose|silent overrides
ai_opportunity: Runs the eval gate automatically on every PR.

name: Pin and version a model+prompt release bundle
description: Snapshot model id, prompt, tools, and config as one pinned, eval-scored release bundle.
general_steps: Snapshot model id + prompt + tools + config|Record the eval scores|Tag the bundle|Link the rollback target
reasoning_model: A model release is a bundle, not a model id; pin everything that affects behavior so you can roll back cleanly.
common_pitfalls: Pinning only the model id|unversioned prompt/config|no recorded scores
ai_opportunity: AI assembles + tags the bundle; PM confirms the release set.

name: Plan a canary or shadow deployment for a model change
description: Plan a canary or shadow rollout for a model/prompt change with online scoring gates.
general_steps: Choose shadow vs canary|Set traffic %|Wire online scorers|Define advance/hold/rollback criteria|Assign a monitor
reasoning_model: A model change is a prod change; ramp it behind online evals, because offline scores never fully predict prod.
common_pitfalls: Big-bang model swaps|no online scoring|no rollback trigger
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the ramp + gates; PM/eng confirm before exposure.

name: Online A/B a model or prompt change with quality and business guardrails
description: Run an online experiment on a model/prompt change against quality and business guardrails.
general_steps: State the hypothesis|Define the online quality metric + guardrails|Compute power/sample size|Pre-set the ship rule|Read out vs criteria
reasoning_model: Offline eval is necessary but not sufficient; the prod distribution decides, so confirm online before full rollout.
common_pitfalls: Shipping on offline scores alone|peeking|no guardrail metrics
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the readout vs criteria; PM owns the ship/kill call.

name: Execute a model or prompt rollback on quality regression
description: Revert to the last-good bundle when online quality regresses.
general_steps: Confirm the regression vs baseline|Select the last-good bundle|Revert|Verify recovery|Log + open a regression case
reasoning_model: Reversible mitigation first; a clean pinned bundle makes rollback a one-click decision under pressure.
common_pitfalls: No pinned last-good|slow rollback|no recovery verification
ai_opportunity: AI detects the regression + prepares the revert; a human authorizes it.

name: Release-readiness go/no-go on eval and safety gates
description: Make the ship call against eval, safety, cost, and rollback-readiness gates.
general_steps: Confirm eval pass vs the bar|Confirm safety/red-team pass|Confirm rollback is ready|Confirm cost in budget|Record go/no-go
reasoning_model: Ship only when the eval, the safety gate, and the rollback all hold; one unchecked critical gate is a no-go.
common_pitfalls: Shipping without a safety pass|no rollback ready|overriding a failed gate
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the gate status; the go/no-go call stays human.

name: Wire online scoring on production traffic
description: Run scorers on a sample of live traffic to monitor quality continuously.
general_steps: Pick online scorers|Set the sampling rate|Build dashboards|Set alert thresholds|Route low scores to triage
reasoning_model: Offline evals catch known failures; online scoring catches the distribution shift you did not anticipate.
common_pitfalls: Offline-only evaluation|no sampling on prod|alert thresholds untuned
ai_opportunity: AI scores prod samples continuously; PM tunes thresholds + routing.

name: Monitor hallucination and groundedness rate
description: Continuously measure whether outputs are grounded in sources (e.g., every number traces to a tool).
general_steps: Define the groundedness check|Score a prod sample|Trend the rate|Alert on a rise|Route ungrounded cases to triage
reasoning_model: Groundedness is the trust metric; for a grounded product, an ungrounded claim is a sev incident, not a quirk.
common_pitfalls: No groundedness metric|trusting fluent output|ignoring slow drift
ai_opportunity: Runs unattended as a groundedness monitor with alerts.

name: Detect quality or behavior drift vs baseline
description: Flag when the system's behavior distribution drifts from its baseline.
general_steps: Set the baseline|Score a rolling window|Flag drift|Segment where it concentrates|Open an RCA
reasoning_model: Models drift even when you change nothing (provider updates, input shift); watch the distribution, not a point.
common_pitfalls: Assuming a frozen model is stable|aggregate-only drift|no segmentation
ai_opportunity: AI flags drift + segments it; PM judges whether it is real.

name: Cluster online failures into a triage queue
description: Auto-score prod outputs, cluster the failures, and prioritize the queue.
general_steps: Auto-score prod outputs|Sort by low score|Cluster failures|Prioritize by frequency x severity|Feed novel ones to the dataset
reasoning_model: Failing prod outputs are the roadmap; cluster into patterns and grow the dataset from them.
common_pitfalls: Reviewing one-by-one|ignoring novel modes|not feeding the dataset
ai_opportunity: AI scores + clusters into a triage queue; PM prioritizes.

name: Build a cost/latency/quality dashboard and per-task cost
description: Track tokens, cost, latency, and quality per task/run in one view.
general_steps: Define the unit (per task/run)|Pull tokens+cost+latency+score|Trend them together|Set alerts|Tie spikes to changes
reasoning_model: The three axes trade off; you cannot manage quality without seeing its cost and latency beside it.
common_pitfalls: Tracking cost without quality|no per-task unit|ignoring latency
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the dashboard; PM reads the trade-offs.

name: Wire human feedback (thumbs/edits) into the flywheel
description: Capture in-product thumbs and edits and route them to the dataset and triage.
general_steps: Capture the feedback signal|Map it to traces|Route to the dataset|Close the loop to the user|Monitor signal volume
reasoning_model: Human feedback is the cheapest label source; capture the edit, not just the thumbs, because the edit shows the fix.
common_pitfalls: Capturing thumbs but not edits|no trace linkage|never using the signal
ai_opportunity: AI routes feedback to the dataset; PM designs the capture + loop.

name: Investigate an eval-score regression (RCA)
description: Root-cause a drop in eval score: is it the model, the judge, the data, or a real regression?
general_steps: Confirm it is real not judge-noise|Diff the changed component (prompt/model/retrieval/tool)|Timestamp-match to changes|Rank hypotheses|Write the verdict
reasoning_model: A score drop has four suspects - model, judge, data, system; eliminate the judge first, because it is cheapest to be wrong.
common_pitfalls: Blaming the model when the judge drifted|correlation-as-cause|stopping at first hypothesis
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the diff + timestamp-matched changes; PM confirms the cause.

name: Reconcile the offline-eval vs online-quality gap
description: Explain why offline eval scores and online quality disagree and fix the eval set.
general_steps: Compare offline scores to online outcomes|Find the distribution shift|Identify missing cases|Add them to the eval set|Re-validate
reasoning_model: When offline says good and prod says bad, the eval set is unrepresentative; the gap is a dataset bug, not a model bug.
common_pitfalls: Trusting offline over online|not updating the eval set|ignoring the shift
ai_opportunity: AI quantifies the gap + proposes additions; PM curates the eval set.

name: Design a red-team campaign
description: Plan structured adversarial probing of the system against a threat model.
general_steps: Define the threat model|Generate attack prompts|Run the campaign|Log breaks|Prioritize fixes + regression cases
reasoning_model: You red-team so you find the jailbreak before users do; absence of a campaign is not absence of vulnerability.
common_pitfalls: No threat model|one-off probing|breaks logged but not fixed
ai_opportunity: AI generates attacks + triages breaks; PM owns the threat model + priorities.

name: Build and maintain a jailbreak and abuse eval corpus
description: Maintain a versioned corpus of jailbreaks and abuse cases to score model resistance.
general_steps: Collect known jailbreaks|Categorize by attack type|Score model resistance|Add new attacks|Version
reasoning_model: Jailbreak resistance is a measurable, regressable property; if it is not in an eval, it will silently regress.
common_pitfalls: Stale corpus|no categorization|treating safety as one-time
ai_opportunity: AI scores resistance against the corpus; PM curates the attack set.

name: Spec guardrail policy and content filters
description: Define what the system must never do and the input/output filters that enforce it.
general_steps: Define disallowed behaviors|Choose input/output filters|Set actions (block/redact/escalate)|Test against the abuse corpus|Document
reasoning_model: Guardrails are policy made executable; the filter that is not tested against real attacks is decorative.
common_pitfalls: Policy with no enforcement|untested filters|over-blocking valid use
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the policy + filter config; PM/safety own the policy.

name: Calibrate refusal and over-refusal rate
description: Tune the system to block harmful requests without refusing valid ones.
general_steps: Build a benign + harmful test set|Measure refuse rates on each|Tune the policy/prompt|Re-measure|Lock the trade-off
reasoning_model: Over-refusal is a real product failure; safety and helpfulness trade off and the balance is a product call.
common_pitfalls: Optimizing harm-blocking only|ignoring over-refusal|no benign set
ai_opportunity: AI measures both rates; PM owns the safety/helpfulness balance.

name: Triage a safety incident or harmful output
description: Run the PM side of a live safety incident: contain, communicate, and capture a regression.
general_steps: Confirm scope + severity|Contain (filter/rollback)|Communicate to stakeholders|Postmortem|Add a regression case
reasoning_model: Contain before you diagnose; a harmful output in prod is reversible only forward, via filter or rollback.
common_pitfalls: Slow containment|no comms|no regression case from the incident
ai_opportunity: AI assembles impact + affected scope; a human owns containment + comms.

name: Run a pre-release safety eval gate
description: Block release on jailbreak, abuse, and PII-leak eval suites failing their floor.
general_steps: Run jailbreak + abuse + PII suites|Compare to the safety floor|Block on fail|Require sign-off|Log
reasoning_model: Safety is a release gate, not a launch-day checklist; a failed safety floor is a hard no-go.
common_pitfalls: Treating safety as advisory|no PII check|overriding a failed floor
ai_opportunity: AI runs the suites; the gate decision stays human.

name: Model portfolio build-vs-buy review
description: Decide which capabilities use which models - API, open-weight, or fine-tuned - with lock-in and data-rights weighed.
general_steps: Map needs to models|Compare API vs open vs fine-tune|Assess lock-in + data rights|Estimate switching cost|Recommend the portfolio
reasoning_model: Model choice is a portfolio decision with lock-in risk; do not single-source a capability you cannot switch.
common_pitfalls: Single-vendor lock-in|ignoring data rights|choosing on hype
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the comparison; PM owns the portfolio + lock-in call.

name: Inference cost-per-task budget and unit economics
description: Model the unit economics of an AI feature: cost per task, margin, and the cost-quality frontier.
general_steps: Define the task unit|Measure tokens x price + tool/infra cost|Model margin per task|Map the cost-quality frontier|Set the budget
reasoning_model: For an AI product, inference is COGS; a feature that is loved and unprofitable per call is a problem, not a win.
common_pitfalls: Ignoring per-task cost|no margin model|optimizing quality with no cost ceiling
ai_opportunity: AI computes per-task cost + frontier; PM sets the budget.

name: Eval roadmap tied to capability and eval gaps
description: Sequence which evals to build next, ranked by risk and use-case value.
general_steps: Inventory eval gaps|Rank by risk x use-case value|Sequence the eval builds|Tie to capability bets|Publish
reasoning_model: The eval roadmap is the product roadmap for an AI system; you can only build what you can grade.
common_pitfalls: Building features ahead of their evals|no risk ranking|orphan evals
ai_opportunity: AI proposes the sequencing from the gap map; PM owns priorities.

name: Data-rights, licensing, and privacy posture for training and traces
description: Set the policy for what data may be used for training/evals, with consent, licensing, and PII handled.
general_steps: Inventory data sources|Check rights/consent/licensing|Classify PII|Set retention + access|Publish the policy
reasoning_model: The flywheel's fuel is user data; using it without rights is a legal and trust failure that compounds.
common_pitfalls: Training on data without rights|no consent for trace reuse|PII in datasets
ai_opportunity: AI inventories sources + flags risks; legal/PM own the posture.

name: Capability roadmap tied to eval gaps
description: Set the AI capability strategy: which bets to make, anchored to eval targets and capability deltas.
general_steps: Synthesize eval gaps + capability deltas|Pick the bets|Tie each to an eval target|Sequence|Write the narrative
reasoning_model: Strategy for an AI product is choosing which capabilities to grade-and-grow next; the insight stays human.
common_pitfalls: Capability bets with no eval target|chasing benchmarks not use cases|template strategy
ai_opportunity: AI stress-tests the strategy; the differentiating bet stays human.

name: Design usage or token-based pricing for an AI product
description: Price an AI product so revenue tracks the inference cost it incurs.
general_steps: Pick the value-aligned usage unit|Map unit to inference cost|Set price + included tier|Define overage + caps|Model margin + bill-shock risk
reasoning_model: Price the unit that tracks both value and cost; flat pricing on metered COGS is how AI products lose money at scale.
common_pitfalls: Flat price on metered cost|bill-shock risk|metering a non-value unit
ai_opportunity: AI models the pricing scenarios; PM/finance own the call.

name: Define AI feature tier-gating and rate limits
description: Decide which AI capabilities, quotas, and model tiers gate which plan.
general_steps: List AI capabilities + their cost|Assign to tiers|Set per-tier rate limits/quotas|Gate premium models to higher tiers|Check competitive parity
reasoning_model: Gate on cost and capability, not just features; rate limits are a margin control, not only a product one.
common_pitfalls: Gating table-stakes capability|no rate limits on costly models|quota mismatch with cost
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the gating matrix; PM validates upgrade + margin logic.

name: Model the gross-margin impact of an AI feature
description: Project how an AI feature's inference COGS moves gross margin at scale.
general_steps: Estimate per-use inference cost|Project usage distribution|Model COGS at scale|Run a cost-sensitivity range|Recommend with guardrails
reasoning_model: Inference COGS scales with usage; a feature's margin can invert as it succeeds, so model the tail.
common_pitfalls: Ignoring COGS at scale|flat usage assumptions|no sensitivity on token growth
ai_opportunity: AI builds the margin model; PM owns the assumptions.

name: Write a GTM or launch brief for an AI capability
description: Position and launch an AI capability around its evaluated capability, trust, and limits.
general_steps: Set the launch tier|Write positioning around capability + trust|State the limits honestly|Plan enablement|Define launch + quality metrics
reasoning_model: Sell the evaluated capability, not the hype; overclaiming an AI capability is a trust debt you pay in churn.
common_pitfalls: Overclaiming capability|hiding limits|no eval evidence behind claims
ai_opportunity: AI drafts positioning from eval evidence; PMM/PM own the message.

name: Triage sales and CS requests for the AI product
description: Triage field requests, separating the model cannot from we have not wired it.
general_steps: Collect requests with context|Dedupe vs known asks|Classify model-limit vs product-gap|Weight by value|Route or decline with reason
reasoning_model: For AI products, half of it does not work is a capability limit, not a backlog item; classify before committing.
common_pitfalls: Promising capability the model lacks|treating limits as quick fixes|no reply to the field
ai_opportunity: AI dedupes + classifies model-limit vs gap; PM owns commitments.

name: Build sales enablement for an AI feature
description: Equip the field to set accurate expectations about an AI feature's accuracy, limits, and guardrails.
general_steps: Summarize what the feature does + its limits|Write the accuracy/trust talk track|Add objection handling (hallucination, data use)|Note guardrails|Distribute
reasoning_model: The field will overpromise unless armed with the limits; expectation-setting is the enablement, not just the value pitch.
common_pitfalls: Value-only pitch with no limits|no objection handling on accuracy/data|stale claims
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the enablement from spec + evals; PM/PMM confirm.

name: Run AI win-loss analysis
description: Analyze why deals were won/lost on AI-specific factors: accuracy, trust, hallucination, latency, cost.
general_steps: Pull won/lost deals|Read notes for AI-specific reasons|Cluster trust/accuracy/latency/cost drivers|Weight by deal size|Tie to eval/roadmap
reasoning_model: AI deals are won and lost on trust and reliability; recurring it hallucinated in the POC is roadmap signal.
common_pitfalls: Missing AI-trust loss reasons|anecdote over pattern|ignoring deal size
ai_opportunity: AI clusters AI-specific win/loss drivers; PM validates causes.

name: Prepare an exec or board update on AI quality and economics
description: Brief leadership on eval trend, cost-per-task, safety posture, and the capability roadmap.
general_steps: Lead with the eval-quality trend|Show cost/run + margin|Surface safety posture + incidents|Tie to the capability roadmap|Pre-empt the hard questions
reasoning_model: For an AI product, the board cares about quality trend, unit economics, and safety exposure; show the eval curve, not feature counts.
common_pitfalls: Feature-dump instead of quality/economics|hiding incidents|no eval trend
ai_opportunity: AI drafts from eval + cost data; PM owns framing + the room.

name: Respond to a why is the AI wrong escalation
description: Handle a stakeholder escalation about AI reliability by reframing expectations with evidence.
general_steps: Acknowledge + get the specific case|Reproduce + classify the failure|Frame it vs the eval/groundedness data|Set realistic reliability expectations|Log + route the fix
reasoning_model: Reframe from it is broken to here is the measured reliability and the fix path; non-determinism needs expectation-setting, not defensiveness.
common_pitfalls: Defensiveness|over-promising perfection|no evidence in the reply
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the repro + reliability evidence; PM owns the relationship-sensitive reply.

name: Author the AI trust and transparency narrative for buyers
description: Write the buyer-facing account of how the system is evaluated, grounded, and kept safe.
general_steps: Inventory what buyers ask about AI|Describe the eval + grounding approach|Document data use + retention|State safety + human-oversight controls|Keep it current
reasoning_model: Buyers now diligence AI trust like security; a clear transparency narrative deflects the AI questionnaire and accelerates deals.
common_pitfalls: Vague claims|overstating safety|stale artifacts
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the narrative from eval/safety docs; PM/security approve publishing.

name: Build an AI adoption and engagement scorecard
description: Track adoption of the AI feature and the acceptance/edit rate of its outputs.
general_steps: Define eligible users|Measure adoption + frequency|Measure acceptance/edit/override rate|Trend vs target|Segment by cohort
reasoning_model: For AI features, the acceptance/edit rate is the truest adoption signal - it shows whether users trust the output.
common_pitfalls: Counting usage not acceptance|ignoring edit rate|no target
ai_opportunity: AI computes adoption + acceptance metrics; PM judges health.

name: Handle AI data-use consent and disclosure in the product
description: Spec the in-product consent for using user data in the flywheel and disclosure of AI involvement.
general_steps: Identify data-use + AI-disclosure points|Spec consent capture + storage|Spec AI-involvement disclosure|Cover opt-out|Review with legal
reasoning_model: Consent for the flywheel and disclosure of AI are trust and compliance gates; a silent data-grab or hidden AI is a liability.
common_pitfalls: Training on data without consent|no AI disclosure|no opt-out
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the consent/disclosure copy + flow; legal/PM own it.

name: Competitive AI capability and pricing teardown
description: Compare competitors on both AI capability (via your evals) and pricing/packaging.
general_steps: List competitors|Run their AI on your eval set|Capture their pricing + limits|Normalize for comparison|Flag capability + pricing gaps
reasoning_model: Compare AI capability on your task and pricing on value; a competitor's leaderboard win may not survive your eval.
common_pitfalls: Comparing on public benchmarks|ignoring pricing model|stale capability read
ai_opportunity: AI runs the teardown on your evals + tabulates pricing; PM judges the response.

name: Verify trace instrumentation
description: Confirm every run logs the fields needed to replay and score it: inputs, full output, tool calls, context, versions, and cost.
general_steps: Audit a sample run|Check inputs + full output + tool calls + context + versions + cost are logged|Map fields to the gen_ai.* OTel convention|File instrumentation gaps
reasoning_model: You cannot evaluate what you did not capture; a run that cannot be replayed and re-scored is invisible to the eval loop.
common_pitfalls: Logging the answer but not the tool calls|missing prompt/model versions|no cost capture|inventing a bespoke schema
ai_opportunity: Auto-audit a sample of runs against the required-field checklist and flag gaps.

name: Pull a smart trace sample for review
description: Assemble ~100 high-yield traces for review, biased to failures and outliers, not a random sample.
general_steps: Choose the window|Bias to negative-feedback + outliers + known clusters|Stratify by segment|Export for review
reasoning_model: Random sampling wastes review on the boring middle; a stratified, failure-biased sample is where the signal lives.
common_pitfalls: Random sampling|ignoring negative feedback|no segment stratification|too-small a window
ai_opportunity: Auto-assemble a stratified, failure-biased sample for human review.

name: Run an open-coding pass
description: Read each trace and write a free-text note on the first thing that went wrong, before any categories exist.
general_steps: Read the full trace|Note the first upstream failure in free text (no categories)|Mark pass/fail|Save the note
reasoning_model: Open coding is the irreducible look-at-your-data step; categories imposed too early blind you to the failure you did not expect.
common_pitfalls: Imposing categories too early|noting the last error not the first|skimming instead of reading|delegating it to an LLM
ai_opportunity: None for the coding itself - this is the human step; AI only assembles the trace and the viewer.

name: Axial-code notes into a failure taxonomy
description: Cluster the open-coding notes into ~5-10 named, defined failure modes.
general_steps: Gather all notes|LLM first-pass clustering|Human-validate the clusters|Write a one-line definition per mode
reasoning_model: The taxonomy is the vocabulary the team argues in; the LLM proposes clusters, the human owns the names and boundaries.
common_pitfalls: Too many modes|overlapping modes|accepting LLM clusters unvalidated|vague definitions
ai_opportunity: AI proposes a first-pass clustering of notes; the human validates and names the modes.

name: Quantify failure rates and triage each mode
description: Label all sampled traces against the taxonomy, count each mode, and decide fix vs assertion vs judge vs guardrail.
general_steps: Label every trace vs the taxonomy|Count the rate per mode|Rank by frequency x severity|Assign each mode to fix / assertion / judge / guardrail
reasoning_model: Counting turns anecdotes into priorities; the triage decision is what routes each mode into the rest of the loop.
common_pitfalls: Acting on an un-counted anecdote|no severity weighting|every mode routed to fix|skipping the monitor decision
ai_opportunity: AI labels traces against the taxonomy and tallies rates; PM owns the triage routing.

name: Build a from-prod failure dataset
description: Turn confirmed production failures into a labeled, versioned dataset of eval cases.
general_steps: One-click add failing traces|Attach the note + label|De-dup|Version and freeze the split
reasoning_model: Confirmed failures are the highest-value eval cases; a frozen labeled split is what proves a fix and prevents the regression.
common_pitfalls: Unlabeled cases|duplicates inflating the set|leakage into training|an unfrozen split that drifts
ai_opportunity: AI proposes additions from the triage queue; PM curates and labels.

name: Write a code or assertion grader
description: Write a deterministic check for a code or structured output - schema, exact-match, execution, or tool-argument.
general_steps: Pick one dimension|Write the check (schema/match/exec/tool-arg)|Unit-test the grader|Register it in the suite
reasoning_model: Use code for what code can verify; a deterministic assertion is cheaper and more trustworthy than any judge.
common_pitfalls: Using a judge where an assertion would do|lumping dimensions|an untested grader|no registration
ai_opportunity: AI writes the assertion code from the dimension spec; PM verifies it checks the right thing.

name: Build and validate an LLM-judge
description: Build a single-dimension LLM judge and validate it against human labels before trusting it.
general_steps: Write the judge prompt + rubric (allow an Unknown verdict)|Collect 50-100 binary human labels|Split 75/25 train/test|Measure TPR/TNR on the held-out labels
reasoning_model: Who validates the validators? A judge is untrusted until it agrees with humans; allowing Unknown stops it from guessing.
common_pitfalls: Trusting an unvalidated judge|no Unknown option|labeling and testing on the same data|a multi-dimension judge
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the judge prompt; PM owns the labels and the TPR/TNR bar.

name: Recalibrate a judge against fresh labels
description: Re-check an in-production judge against fresh human labels to catch judge-human drift.
general_steps: Sample recent judged items|Re-label a gold subset|Recompute TPR/TNR or kappa|Adjust the judge prompt
reasoning_model: A judge drifts as the input distribution moves; an uncalibrated judge silently stops measuring what you think.
common_pitfalls: Never recalibrating|too-small a gold subset|adjusting without re-measuring|ignoring the drift
ai_opportunity: AI recomputes agreement and proposes prompt fixes; PM owns the fresh gold labels.

name: Generate and validate structured synthetic data
description: Bootstrap an eval set when no production data exists, then validate realism and leakage.
general_steps: Define the input dimensions|Hand-write ~20 seed tuples|Two-step generate from the seeds|Validate realism + check leakage vs eval
reasoning_model: Synthetic data unblocks a cold start, but unvalidated it is contamination; hand-write the seeds so the dimensions are real.
common_pitfalls: No seed examples|mode-collapsed generations|leakage into the eval set|skipping the realism check
ai_opportunity: AI generates from the seeds; PM validates realism and leakage.

name: Curate golden and adversarial sets; version splits
description: Maintain the stable golden bar plus an adversarial/incident-repro set, with versioned splits.
general_steps: Sample representative + hard cases|Minimize incident repros into cases|Label the expected behavior|Freeze the versioned split
reasoning_model: The golden set is the stable bar; the adversarial set is the immune memory; both must be frozen to stay comparable.
common_pitfalls: Mixing golden and adversarial|unfrozen splits|no incident repros|leakage
ai_opportunity: AI proposes candidate cases; PM curates the golden and adversarial splits.

name: Configure eval-gated CI (assertions then judges)
description: Block merges that regress the eval suite: assertions first, judges where needed, thresholds, deltas commented on the PR.
general_steps: Wire the suite to the PR|Run assertions first, judges where needed|Set the thresholds|Fail the build + comment the deltas
reasoning_model: The eval is the unit test for a non-deterministic system; if it is not in CI it is not a gate.
common_pitfalls: Eval not in CI|thresholds too loose|judges where assertions suffice|silent overrides
ai_opportunity: Runs the eval gate automatically on every PR.

name: Wire online scoring and drift alerts
description: Score a sample of production traffic with reference-free scorers and alert when the lower-bound CI breaches a floor.
general_steps: Pick reference-free scorers|Set the sampling rate|Build dashboards|Alert on the lower-bound CI breaching the floor
reasoning_model: Production has no reference answer, so score reference-free; alert on the CI lower bound, not the point estimate, to avoid noise.
common_pitfalls: Alerting on the point estimate|no sampling discipline|reference-based scorers in prod|untuned thresholds
ai_opportunity: AI scores prod samples continuously; PM tunes scorers and thresholds.

name: Investigate an eval-score regression (RCA)
description: Root-cause an eval-score drop: distinguish judge variance from a real regression, then localize the component.
general_steps: Confirm it is real, not judge variance|Diff the changed component (prompt/model/retrieval/tool)|Timestamp-match to deploys|Write the verdict
reasoning_model: A score drop has four suspects - model, judge, data, system; eliminate judge noise first because it is the cheapest way to be fooled.
common_pitfalls: Blaming the model when the judge drifted|correlation-as-cause|no timestamp match|stopping at the first hypothesis
ai_opportunity: AI assembles the diff and timestamp-matched changes; PM confirms the cause.

name: Build the agent transition-failure matrix
description: Localize where multi-step agent runs break by tallying last-success against first-failure states.
general_steps: Define the agent states|Tally last-success x first-failure across runs|Rank the transition hotspots|Link exemplar traces
reasoning_model: A drop-off in an agent is a transition failure, not a funnel step; the matrix shows which hand-off actually breaks.
common_pitfalls: Reading the aggregate not the transition|ill-defined states|no exemplar links|ignoring rare-but-fatal transitions
ai_opportunity: AI tallies the transition matrix from traces; PM reads the hotspots.

name: Plan eval-gated canary/shadow and rollback
description: Graduate a model or prompt change safely: pin the bundle, shadow then canary, gate on online scores, define the rollback rule.
general_steps: Pin the bundle (model + prompt + tools)|Shadow, then canary a traffic %|Wire online scorers|Set the advance / rollback rule
reasoning_model: A model change is a prod change gated on online quality; ungraded autonomous rollout is the dangerous quadrant - pin so rollback is one click.
common_pitfalls: Big-bang rollout|no online scoring|an unpinned bundle|no rollback rule
ai_opportunity: AI drafts the ramp + gates and watches the scorers; a human authorizes advance or rollback.
